name: Cranberry Cheesecake with Cranberry Orange Co
recipeIngredient: 1 lb (about 4 2/3 cups) fresh or frozen cranberries
2 lbs cream cheese, at room temperature
1 3/4 cups reserved cranberry filling from cheesecake recipe
2 tsp minced orange peel (only the zest, no white pith)
recipeInstructions:
- CRANBERRY FILLING: Combine cranberries, sugar, and orange juice in a heavy medium saucepan. Bring to boil over medium-high heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat and simmer until berries pop and mixture thickens (about 10 minutes). Cool completely.
- CHEESE FILLING: Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese and sugar in a large bowl until well blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition. Set aside.
- CRUST: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly butter the bottom and sides of a 9-inch diameter spring form pan (2 3/4 inches high). Place crumbs in a medium bowl. Add melted butter and blend until moist crumbs form. Press onto the bottom of prepared pan. Pour cheese filling over crust. Spoon about 1 cup of cranberry filling over the cheese filling. Using a small sharp knife, swirl to create a marble pattern. Bake until center is set (about 1 hour). Transfer cheesecake to a rack and maintain oven temperature.
- TOPPING: Mix sour cream, sugar, and vanilla in a bowl. Gently press down any raised edges of the cheesecake. Spoon topping over the cake. Return to oven for an additional 5 minutes. Transfer cheesecake to a rack and cool completely. Cover and refrigerate overnight (cheesecake can be prepared up to 2 days in advance). Release pan sides. Transfer cheesecake to a platter. Cut into wedges and serve, passing compote separately.
- CRANBERRY ORANGE COMPOTE: Combine sugar and water in a heavy small saucepan. Bring to boil, stirring until sugar dissolves and liquid is clear. Transfer to a medium bowl and mix in reserved cranberry filling and minced orange zest. Using a sharp knife, cut away the white pith from the oranges. Working over another bowl to catch juices, cut between membranes to release segments. Add segments with juices to the compote (can be prepared up to 8 hours ahead). Add orange segments and juices to the compote and serve.
